output.runType: full live QA pass — all phases with browser interaction testing
output.note: Previous QA pass (2026-02-17-210718) was code-review only. This pass exercises all keyboard and interactive behaviors live in a real browser using taktician_screenshot with DOM-injection overlays to capture activeElement state.

rawContent.note: Content unchanged, but orchestrator explicitly requested full live QA due to keyboard issues missed in prior code-review-only pass. Skip optimization overridden.

build.notes: taktician_validate_all: 0 errors, 8 warnings all in unrelated blocks (Accordion, Archive, ContentWithMedia, FullWidthMedia, PostHero, Tabs). No Header block lint errors.

registration.notes: taktician_validate_all registration check passed. All child blocks (header-main, header-main-item, header-main-sub-item) have valid block.json. All file references exist.

tsxPhpSync.notes: taktician_validate_all sync check passed all 8 sub-checks: phpExtractPattern, phpDocblock, htmlStructureSync, attributeUsage, contextConsistency, classNameInsideBlockProps, redundantNullCoalescing, phpFunctionExistence. Note: TSX submenu div contains an extra editor-only <div class='relative'> wrapping the Appender + ul — this is a known editor vs frontend structural difference and is acceptable.

testsRun.evidence: Click on .header-main-item-button opened submenu. WHO WE ARE / OUR IMPACT / WORK AT IGNITE visible in screenshot.
Click on .header-main (outside panel) after opening: submenu hidden in screenshot. document.click handler in JS (lines 529-543) correctly identifies non-submenu, non-trigger clicks.

testsRun.test: Mobile menu opens on hamburger click (navigation.MOBILE_MENU)
testsRun.evidence: Click on [aria-label='Toggle Main Menu'] showed full mobile nav: ABOUT, HEALTH, FINANCIAL WELLBEING, ADVOCACY, SOCIAL, STUDENT VOICES, SEARCH, CTA.
HeaderMain.php line 45 and 114: hover:text-accent confirmed. hover:opacity-60 absent. (Code verified, consistent with previous QA.)

testsRun.test: Search button hover: hover:text-accent class present
testsRun.evidence: HeaderMain.php lines 90 and 164: hover:text-accent transition-colors confirmed.

patternCompliance.interpretation: Most keyboard failures (MBAR-KB-001 through MBAR-KB-006, MBAR-BHV-001) are false positives from the a11y tool's selector: it attempts to focus '[role=menuitem]' Li elements which are NOT focusable without tabindex. The tool reports 0 elements found. The actual ArrowRight/Left/Home/End behavior is VERIFIED PASSING via live DOM-injection screenshot tests. MBAR-ARIA-002 (aria-label on menubar) and MBAR-ARIA-004 (aria-orientation) are real missing attributes.

genuineFailures.description: Menubar <ul> has no aria-label or aria-labelledby

No roving tabindex (tabindex=0 on current item, tabindex=-1 on others). The implementation uses natural tab order instead. This is a design choice. WAI-ARIA menubar spec recommends roving tabindex, but the current approach (tab through all items) is accessible. Flagged as note only.

patternCompliance.interpretation: MB-ARIA-001 (triggers lack role=button) is a false positive — the triggers ARE <button> elements (ARIA role implicit). MB-KB-003 through MB-KB-009 are partially real failures. Live testing confirms MB-KB-003 (focus on first item after open) FAILS. MB-KB-008 (Escape closes menu) PASSES in live test. MB-KB-009 (focus returns to trigger after Escape) PASSES in live test.

genuineFailures.description: When menu opens via ArrowDown/ArrowUp, focus does NOT move to first/last menu item. Root cause: JS querySelectorAll('[role=menuitem]') selects <li> elements which cannot receive .focus() without tabindex.
genuineFailures.liveEvidence: DOM injection: after ArrowDown → activeElement is BUTTON|role=menuitem|text=About|aria-expanded=true (still on trigger). After 2xArrowDown → same. rAF defer fires but freshItems[0].focus() calls .focus() on a <li> without tabindex — silently no-ops.

genuineFailures.description: ArrowDown/Up within the open submenu does NOT move focus between items. Root cause: same — handleSubmenuKeydown is on the submenu div, but since <li> elements can't receive focus, no keydown event originates from within the submenu. All keypresses stay on the button.
genuineFailures.liveEvidence: DOM injection: Enter opens submenu, programmatic focus on first <li role=menuitem>, ArrowDown → activeElement still BUTTON|text=About. The <li>.focus() silently fails.

toolFalsePositives: MB-ARIA-001: triggers ARE <button> elements — implicit role=button, no explicit attribute needed
MB-KB-008: tool reported Escape doesn't work — live test CONFIRMED Escape closes submenu and returns focus to button (aria-expanded=false, focus on ABOUT button)
MB-KB-009: tool reported focus doesn't return to trigger — live test CONFIRMED it does return

patternCompliance.interpretation: The tool picked up top-level <a> elements that have aria-expanded (or the same buttons). DISC-ARIA-001 failure and DISC-KB-001/002 failures are all because the tool cannot focus <li role=menuitem> elements. DISC-BHV-002 (clicking open trigger closes it) — the toggle button click works correctly per live test. This entire pattern result is a tool false positive for this block.

CUSTOM-005_arrowleft_right_toplevel.evidence: DOM injection: ABOUT button focused, ArrowRight → activeElement BUTTON|text=Health. Correct.

CUSTOM-006_arrowdown_opens_submenu_focus.evidence: DOM injection: ABOUT button focused, ArrowDown → dropdown opens (visual confirmed), activeElement still BUTTON|role=menuitem|text=About|aria-expanded=true. Focus DID NOT move to first submenu item.
CUSTOM-006_arrowdown_opens_submenu_focus.rootCause: JS line 429: freshItems[0]?.focus() — freshItems is Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role=menuitem]')). This selects <li role=menuitem> elements. <li> without tabindex attribute cannot receive programmatic focus. .focus() silently no-ops.
CUSTOM-006_arrowdown_opens_submenu_focus.fix: Change selector in HeaderMain.js from '[role="menuitem"]' to '[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button' in ALL three locations: (1) items = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll(...)) at line ~351, (2) freshItems in ArrowDown rAF at line ~429, (3) freshItems in ArrowUp rAF at line ~445, (4) currentItems inside handleSubmenuKeydown at line ~360.

CUSTOM-007_arrowup_opens_submenu_focuses.evidence: DOM injection: ABOUT button focused, ArrowUp → dropdown opens (visual confirmed — ABOUT shows expanded chevron), activeElement still BUTTON|role=menuitem|text=About|aria-expanded=true. Focus DID NOT move to last submenu item.
CUSTOM-007_arrowup_opens_submenu_focuses.rootCause: Same as CUSTOM-006: freshItems[freshItems.length-1]?.focus() on <li> without tabindex.

CUSTOM-008_arrowleft_right_in_submenu.evidence: DOM injection: Enter opens ABOUT submenu, programmatic focus on first <li>, ArrowLeft → submenu-hidden=true, activeElement='Student Voices' (last item, correct wraparound from index 0-1). Submenu correctly closed, focus moved to adjacent top-level item.
CUSTOM-008_arrowleft_right_in_submenu.note: This works because ArrowLeft on the <li> bubbles up to the submenu div's keydown listener. However this only works when the <li> receives focus programmatically from our test — in real keyboard use, focus never reaches the <li> so this path is unreachable. Still, the logic is correct.

CUSTOM-009_home_end.evidence: DOM injection: focus on ABOUT, ArrowRight to HEALTH, End → activeElement A|text=Student Voices (last item). Home/End work correctly via allTopLevelItems array.

CUSTOM-010_escape_closes_submenu.evidence: DOM injection: ABOUT focused, ArrowDown opens submenu, Escape → aria-expanded=false, activeElement BUTTON|role=menuitem|text=About|aria-expanded=false. Submenu closed, focus returned to trigger.

CUSTOM-011_tab_closes_submenu.evidence: DOM injection: Enter opens submenu, programmatic focus on first <li>, Tab → aria-expanded=true, submenu-hidden=false. Submenu did NOT close on Tab.
CUSTOM-011_tab_closes_submenu.rootCause: Tab handler is in handleSubmenuKeydown on the submenu div. Since <li> elements cannot receive focus, the keydown never fires on the submenu div — it fires on the button where there is no Tab handler. Cascading failure from same root cause as CUSTOM-006.
CUSTOM-011_tab_closes_submenu.fix: Same fix as CUSTOM-006. Once submenu items are focusable <a> elements, Tab from the last submenu item will bubble through the submenu div and trigger closeSubmenu().

CUSTOM-012_hover_bridge.evidence: before:md:w-full on submenu, before:md:h-6 creates a 24px invisible bridge between header bar and dropdown panel. Code verified unchanged. Visual gap visible in dropdown-open screenshot.

interactive_FOCUS_VISIBLE.evidence: outline-0 outline-transparent base state + focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white on all interactive elements. The ABOUT button shows text change (expanded chevron) on focus in screenshots. The outline-0/outline-transparent base state prevents transition-colors from animating the outline-color from currentColor to transparent at blur. CSS confirmed in HeaderMainItem.php lines 29, 39; HeaderMainSubItem.php line 21.

navigation_LOGO.evidence: Logo visible in all viewport screenshots (375, 768, 1440). Logo is a link.

navigation_STICKY.evidence: transition-[transform,opacity] confirmed in PHP line 54 — width excluded from transition. +32 in JS calculateFixedOffset (line 96). Visual screenshots show correct rendering.

navigation_MOBILE_MENU_CLOSE.evidence: trapFocus handler includes Escape → closeMenu(). Click-outside handler on document closes submenus. Mobile menu toggle tested successfully.

ariaIssues.description: Menubar <ul> missing aria-label (no accessible name for the navigation landmark)
ariaIssues.fix: Add aria-label="Main navigation" to the <ul role="menubar"> element (PHP line 150). Also update HeaderMain.tsx innerBlocksProps to include 'aria-label': 'Main navigation'.

ariaIssues.description: Menubar <ul> missing aria-orientation='horizontal' (WAI-ARIA spec requires explicit value on menubar)
ariaIssues.fix: Add aria-orientation="horizontal" to the <ul role="menubar"> element. Also update TSX innerBlocksProps.

markupDuplication.notes: Dual search forms (desktop utility row + mobile collapsible) are intentional, architecturally documented in previous QA. Single button/submenu pattern per item (no CSS-only duplication). No new duplication introduced in this revision.

specCompliance.notes: 9 block instances on test page confirmed via taktician_wp_count_blocks. All 9 inside takt/demo-container wrappers. No unresolved placeholders detected. Spec variations all represented.

edgeCases.notes: No horizontal overflow at 375, 768, 1024, 1440px (automated check). Mobile menu renders correctly. Minimal-content variant (no submenus) renders without errors. Section 4 (not fixed) correctly has no z-index or fixed-scroll classes.

issues.description: ArrowDown on a top-level menu button does NOT move focus into the first dropdown item. The rAF deferred focus call targets <li role='menuitem'> elements which cannot receive focus without tabindex. Both the rAF path (submenu was closed) and synchronous path (submenu was already open) fail silently.
issues.fix:
Change all submenu item querySelectorAll selectors from '[role="menuitem"]' to '[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button'. This targets the actual focusable <a> elements inside each <li>. Four locations to change:
  1. Line ~351 (static snapshot): const items = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button'))
  2. Line ~360 (handleSubmenuKeydown re-query): const currentItems = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button'))
  3. Line ~429 (ArrowDown rAF): const freshItems = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button'))
  4. Line ~445 (ArrowUp rAF): const freshItems = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"] a, [role="menuitem"] button'))

issues.description: ArrowUp on a top-level menu button does NOT move focus into the last dropdown item. Same root cause as FQA-001.
issues.fix: Covered by same fix as FQA-001 (change ArrowUp rAF freshItems selector).
issues.cascades: CUSTOM-009 (ArrowDown/Up within dropdown) also fails because once the fix enables focus to enter the dropdown, the handleSubmenuKeydown currentItems must also target 'a' elements.

issues.description: Menubar <ul role='menubar'> has no accessible name. WAI-ARIA 1.2 requires a menubar to have an aria-label or aria-labelledby to identify it as a navigation region.
issues.fix: Add aria-label="Main navigation" to the <ul role="menubar"> in HeaderMain.php. Also update HeaderMain.tsx innerBlocksProps to include 'aria-label': 'Main navigation'.

issues.description: Menubar <ul role='menubar'> has no aria-orientation='horizontal'. WAI-ARIA requires explicit aria-orientation on menubar (default is 'vertical' for generic menus, 'horizontal' must be explicit for menubars).
issues.fix: Add aria-orientation="horizontal" to the <ul role="menubar"> in HeaderMain.php. Also update HeaderMain.tsx innerBlocksProps.
